records.ety: [OE. gliteren; akin to Sw. glittra, Icel. glitra, glita, AS. glitenian, OS. glītan, OHG. glīzzan, G. gleissen, Goth. glitmunjan, and also to E. glint, glisten, and prob. glance, gleam.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Glittered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Glittering.]

defs: To sparkle with light; to shine with a brilliant and broken light or showy luster; to gleam; as, a glittering sword.
quotes: The field yet glitters with the pomp of war.

defs: To be showy, specious, or striking, and hence attractive; as, the glittering scenes of a court.
A bright, sparkling light; brilliant and showy luster; brilliancy; as, the glitter of arms; the glitter of royal equipage.
